concerns.text: The 'ANOVA (Functional)' dataset cited (URL: .../functional_anova_2026-05-19.json) appears to be a results file (JSON) rather than a raw tabular dataset suitable for re-running statistical tests. The plan requires 'Ground Truth Establishment' on clean data to inject errors. A pre-computed results file cannot be corrupted or re-analyzed. This dataset is inappropriate for the simulation pipeline described.

The 'UCI DROP' dataset is listed as 'Text/Numerical' and requires 'feature extraction' to fit statistical tests. The plan's error injection logic (Section 3) targets specific column types (numerical replacement, categorical misclassification). Relying on ad-hoc feature extraction from text data introduces uncontrolled variability and may not yield the clean, typed columns required for the deterministic error injection and statistical testing pipeline defined in FR-001/FR-003.

The 'MCAR (Malawi)' and 'MCAR (UNICEF)' datasets are cited with HuggingFace URLs pointing to specific parquet shards (e.g., 'train-00000-of-00001'). These are likely subsets of larger datasets. The plan requires '5-10 diverse public datasets' with sufficient sample sizes (N>30) for parametric tests. Using single shards without verifying the full dataset schema or ensuring the shard contains the necessary variable types (e.g., specific categorical labels for Chi-squared) risks the pipeline failing to find valid test configurations, violating the 'Comprehensive Test Coverage' requirement.
